turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, I woke up yesterday morning and noticed these small red bumps all over both my arms. They're incredibly itchy, and it's been keeping me up at night. I haven't changed any soaps or detergents recently, so I'm not sure what's going on.
When did this start?
It started yesterday morning, like I said. I first noticed it when I was getting dressed, and by the afternoon it seemed to spread across both arms pretty quickly. It's been constant since then.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's not really painful — more like a really intense, constant itching. It feels like a prickly, scratchy sensation all over my forearms and up to my elbows, especially on the inner sides where the skin is softer. The itching gets worse at night and that's when I can't stop scratching, which makes it redder and more irritated.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills. Just the rash and the itching. I felt completely normal apart from that, honestly.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not on any prescription medications right now. I sometimes take ibuprofen if I get a headache or cramps, but I haven't taken any in the past few days.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic conditions. I'm generally pretty healthy, just the occasional cold or something.
Are you allergic to any medications?

I'm allergic to penicillin — I found that out when I was a kid. It made my lips and tongue swell up a bit. Nothing else that I know of, though I'm always a little cautious with new medicines because of that.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've tried a couple of things, but nothing's really helped. I put some calamine lotion on it, and that gave me maybe five minutes of relief before it started itching again. I've also been trying not to scratch, but honestly, I gave in a few times, especially last night. A cold compress helped a little bit, but not for very long.
Does anything make it better or worse?

The cold compress seems to calm it down for a few minutes, but that's about it. Scratching definitely makes it worse — makes the bumps look angrier and more swollen. Also, I've noticed it feels more intense when I get warm, like under my blankets at night or after a hot shower.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been really frustrating. I can't focus on my classes because I'm constantly thinking about the itching, and last night I barely slept — I kept waking up scratching. I'm also pretty self-conscious about how it looks, so I've been wearing long sleeves even though it's warm out, just to cover it up.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke at all. I do drink alcohol occasionally, maybe a couple of drinks with friends on weekends, but I haven't had anything in the past week or so. I didn't think it was related.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, not that I know of. My mom has some seasonal allergies and gets hay fever, but nothing like a skin rash or itching. Nobody else in my family has dealt with anything like this, as far as I'm aware.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. This is the first time I've had a rash like this. I've had dry skin in the winter sometimes, but nothing that itched like this or showed up so suddenly like these red bumps.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, it makes sense. I think I understand what you're saying about it possibly being an allergic reaction or something, but I'm still not sure what caused it since I haven't changed anything. Is there something you'd recommend to help with the itching?
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's around a 6 maybe? The itching is constant and annoying, but it's not super painful. Emotionally, probably a 7 or 8 — I'm really frustrated and tired from the lack of sleep, and I feel a bit anxious because I don't know what's causing it or how long it'll last.
Do you have any questions for me?
I do have a couple of questions. First, what do you think is causing this rash? And second, is there anything stronger I can use to stop the itching so I can actually sleep tonight?
What do you think might be going on?

Honestly, I'm not sure. I keep wondering if it's some kind of allergic reaction to something I touched or ate, but I can't think of anything new. I've also heard about stress rashes — I'm a student and exams are coming up, so maybe that's it? But I really don't know, and it's driving me a little crazy not understanding why this is happening.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Actually, I just remembered — I've been under quite a bit of stress lately with finals coming up. My roommate also mentioned that I've been grinding my teeth in my sleep, which I never used to do. I don't know if that's connected, but I thought you should know. That's all I can think of.
